label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's analyze this question. I need to determine why the text includes so many figures. The question asks what the purpose of the figures is. The text is about credit card usage in China and its implications. The author cites percentages and growth rates to illustrate the situation. For example, "about 30 percent of Chinese urban households now own at least one credit card," "the growth rate of credit card adoption has been an average of 40 percent per year," "more than 90 percent of non-credit card users were unaware of safety issues," "only 55 percent were able to pay the full balance," etc. Let me evaluate each option: (A) Record the valuable data: This option suggests the figures are simply being recorded for future use. However, the figures in the text are used to make a point about the implications of credit card usage, not just recorded in a neutral way. (B) Provide concrete evidence: This option suggests the figures are used to support arguments or claims. The text uses the figures to support claims about the growth of credit card usage, the lack of financial knowledge, and the implications for the economy. This seems to match well. (C) Introduce a research method: The text does not explain how the data was collected or the methodology used. It just presents findings, so this is unlikely. (D) Give a detailed description: While the figures do describe the situation in detail, the main purpose seems to be to support specific arguments rather than just to describe.
